title: linaria viscosa var. salzmannii
intro: Linaria viscosa var. salzmannii, also known as sticky toadflax, is a plant species within the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and wounds, due to its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The sticky toadflax is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
